_template.name: OpenGRIS: Multi-Cloud (3-Tier Waterfall)
_template.description: Enterprise deployment with three-tier waterfall — native on-premise (priority 1), AWS ECS burst (priority 2), and Symphony HPC overflow (priority 3). Maximum elasticity across cloud providers.
_template.author: CalmStudio Contributors

nodes.name: Enterprise Scheduler
nodes.description: OpenGRIS Scaler scheduler orchestrating compute across three worker manager tiers — native, ECS, and Symphony

nodes.name: On-Premise Worker Manager
nodes.description: Native worker manager — first tier in the 3-tier waterfall, handles on-premise compute with lowest latency (priority 1)

nodes.name: ECS Burst Worker Manager
nodes.description: AWS ECS worker manager — second tier in the 3-tier waterfall, handles burst overflow from on-premise (priority 2)

nodes.name: Symphony HPC Worker Manager
nodes.description: IBM Symphony worker manager — third tier in the 3-tier waterfall, handles high-volume HPC overflow from ECS (priority 3)

nodes.name: Enterprise Object Store
nodes.description: Shared object storage for task payloads, intermediate results, and outputs across all compute tiers

nodes.name: Enterprise Client
nodes.description: Enterprise application client submitting high-volume computation jobs to the multi-cloud Scaler deployment

relationships.description: Enterprise client submits computation jobs to the scheduler (ZeroMQ TCP)
Scheduler reads task inputs and writes results via shared object store
Native worker manager registers with scheduler as first waterfall tier; spawns on-premise workers
ECS worker manager registers with scheduler as second waterfall tier; launches ECS tasks on demand
Symphony worker manager registers with scheduler as third waterfall tier; submits jobs to Symphony cluster
